skill.name: skill-share
skill.description: A skill that creates new Claude skills and automatically shares them on Slack using Rube for seamless team collaboration and skill discovery.
skill.summary: Create Claude skills, package them, and announce them to team Slack channels through Rube.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: The static external command and weak cryptography findings are false positives based on the reviewed SKILL.md context. The real risks are the described Slack sharing workflow and local skill packaging, which may disclose internal skill metadata or package unintended files if users configure it carelessly.

medium_findings.title: Slack Sharing Can Disclose Skill Metadata
medium_findings.description: The skill describes automatically sending created skill names, descriptions, summaries, and direct links to Slack through Rube. This is legitimate for team discovery, but it can expose internal details if the target channel or workspace is misconfigured.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented behavior clearly includes Slack posting and sharing direct links. The risk depends on configuration, so the finding is medium rather than high.

medium_findings.title: Packaging Workflow Requires Filesystem Boundaries
medium_findings.description: The skill describes creating directories, generating asset folders, and packaging skill files into distributable zip files. This is expected behavior, but users should review included files and limit write access to avoid publishing unrelated local content.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly requires write access and packaging of assets. No malicious intent is shown, but the workflow has a clear data handling risk.

low_findings.title: False Positive: External Command Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the reviewed lines are a fenced example describing expected steps. No executable shell command, Ruby backtick execution, or command injection vector is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is plain documentation inside a code fence and does not invoke a command. No user-controlled command construction is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Patterns
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptography indicators, but the reviewed lines are ordinary prose about skill creation, packaging, and Slack sharing. No cryptographic API, hash selection, cipher, or password handling appears in the file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain no cryptographic operations or security-sensitive algorithms. The matches appear to be substring or keyword noise in documentation text.

content.value_statement: Teams need a repeatable way to create usable AI skills and make them visible to coworkers. This skill guides skill creation, validation, packaging, and Slack announcements through Rube.
actual_capabilities: Guides creation of a SKILL.md file with required metadata.
Defines standard scripts, references, and assets directories for a new skill.
Checks skill naming and metadata before packaging.
Packages created skill files into a distributable zip archive.
Posts skill names, descriptions, summaries, and links to Slack through Rube.
Uses Rube Slack actions to find channels and send formatted announcements.
limitations: Requires a connected Slack workspace through Rube.
Requires write access to the selected skill creation directory.
Does not provide the actual implementation scripts in this package.
Users must review packaged files before sharing them.

use_cases.title: Publish Internal Skills
use_cases.description: Create a reusable skill and announce it to the team channel for discovery.

use_cases.title: Standardize Skill Structure
use_cases.description: Generate a consistent SKILL.md file and supporting folders before distribution.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Team Contributions
use_cases.description: Share new skill metadata and links with collaborators after validation.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new Claude skill named invoice-helper. Use this description: Extract key invoice fields and prepare a clean summary.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a simple internal skill.

prompt_templates.title: Validate Before Sharing
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a skill named support-triage, validate the SKILL.md metadata, and tell me what should be fixed before packaging.

prompt_templates.title: Package for Distribution
prompt_templates.prompt: Create and package a skill named research-brief-builder. Include references and assets folders, then summarize the files that will be included.

prompt_templates.title: Announce Through Slack
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a skill named release-note-writer, validate it, package it, and prepare a Slack announcement for the ai-tools channel.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow using Rube and Slack sharing.

output_examples.input: Create a skill named pdf-analyzer for extracting PDF summaries.
output_examples.output: A new skill structure is prepared with a SKILL.md file, supporting folders, validation notes, and packaging guidance.

output_examples.input: Package the research helper skill and share it with the team.
output_examples.output: The skill is validated, packaged for distribution, and summarized for a Slack announcement with its name, purpose, and link.

output_examples.input: Check whether my skill is ready to share.
output_examples.output: The response lists metadata status, naming issues, missing folders, and recommended fixes before team publication.

best_practices: Review skill descriptions and included files before sending Slack announcements.
Use a dedicated internal Slack channel for skill publication.
Limit write access to the directory where new skills are created.
anti_patterns: Do not package broad directories that may contain unrelated files.
Do not post private skill links to public or external Slack channels.
Do not skip validation when sharing a skill with other users.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates a structured Claude skill with metadata, standard folders, validation steps, packaging guidance, and Slack sharing support.

faq.question: Does it require Slack?
faq.answer: Slack is required for the announcement workflow. Skill creation and validation can still be described without posting a message.

faq.question: What is Rube used for?
faq.answer: Rube provides Slack actions for finding channels and sending skill announcements.

faq.question: Can it publish skills outside my team?
faq.answer: The skill is designed for team sharing. Users should choose private internal channels for sensitive or early work.

faq.question: Does this package include implementation scripts?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed package contains SKILL.md instructions, but no separate script files.

faq.question: What should I check before sharing?
faq.answer: Check the skill name, metadata, included files, destination channel, and any links that will appear in Slack.
